data.title: Hong Kong will legislate a reporting framework for crypto assets, aiming to complete it by 2026.
data.description: At the 2026 Annual Tax Seminar of the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) Hong Kong Branch, Hong Kong's Under Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Chan Ho-lim, stated that the OECD has revised its Common Reporting Standard (CRS) to include new digital financial products such as central bank digital currencies and optimized the reporting and due diligence requirements for financial institutions. Furthermore, the OECD has released the Crypto Asset Reporting Framework (CARF) to enable tax jurisdictions to automatically exchange crypto asset transaction data, improving transaction transparency. The Hong Kong Legislative Council has given support to complete legislation by 2026. (Aastocks)

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin's "safe-haven" myth fails amid US-Iran conflict; ETF funds reshape pricing logic.
data.description: 10x Research published an article on its X platform stating that against the backdrop of the current US-Iran conflict, Bitcoin has not demonstrated its "anti-inflation" or "safe-haven asset" attributes; instead, it has fallen in tandem with other risky assets, indicating a shift in its price-driving logic. The launch of Bitcoin ETFs has introduced a new group of investors to the market, mostly from Wall Street, who focus more on macroeconomic variables than on-chain applications or network growth indicators. However, not all "macro" indicators apply to Bitcoin. Some retail investors still rely on narratives of "four-year cycles" or even extended to "five-year cycles," leading to continued long positions during the decline. Currently, the market generally misinterprets Bitcoin, viewing it as a safe-haven asset, over-relying on ineffective liquidity models, and ignoring the key macroeconomic factors that truly drive the cycle.

data.title: Gauntlet: Resolv has not yet announced a remedy plan; a claims agreement will be established if assets are recovered.
data.description: DeFi research and risk management firm Gauntlet posted on the X platform that Resolv Labs has not released a remediation plan after the exploit incident, forcing Gauntlet to seek full recovery of funds through various means. To minimize the impact, Gauntlet has removed several vault markets. If assets are recovered, claims agreements will be established for affected liquidity providers. Current key measures include: 1. The wstUSR/USDC market has been removed from the mainnet USDC Core (v1), affecting approximately $7.6 million in liquidity; 2. The wstUSR/USDC, PT-RLP-9APR2026/USDC, and RLP/USDC markets have been removed from USDC Frontier (v1.1), affecting approximately $4.3 million in liquidity; 3. The USR/USDC market has been removed from Seamless USDC (v1.1) and Extrafi XLend USDC (v1.1); 4. The markets related to Resolv USDC (v1.1) will be removed after the 3-day lock-up period ends.

data.title: CoinShares has transferred 10,720 BTC to a new wallet in the past two days, worth over $700 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, CoinShares has transferred 10,720 BTC to a new wallet in the past two days, worth approximately $720 million, marking the largest BTC outflow from the institution.

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Charges Executives in Crypto Wash Trading Case
data.description:
On April 1, the U.S. Justice Department's Northern California Prosecutor's Office announced charges against ten executives and employees from four cryptocurrency market-making firms. According to BlockBeats, the individuals are accused of artificially inflating token trading volumes and prices through wash trading. The firms involved include Gotbit, Vortex, Antier, and Contrarian.

Three of the defendants have been extradited from Singapore to the United States, while two others have pleaded guilty and received sentences. The FBI and the IRS Criminal Investigation Division conducted undercover operations, even creating multiple cryptocurrencies to investigate illegal volume boosting and price manipulation within the industry. Authorities have seized over $1 million in crypto assets as part of the investigation.

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Hints at Upcoming Cuba Developments
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio announced that more information regarding Cuba will be released soon. According to BlockBeats, Rubio stated that efforts are currently underway to address the situation. Further details are expected to be disclosed in the near future.

data.title: Earthquake Strikes Southern Ibaraki Prefecture, Shaking Felt in Tokyo
data.description: A magnitude 5 earthquake struck the southern part of Ibaraki Prefecture in Japan, with noticeable tremors felt in Tokyo. According to RTHK, the Japan Meteorological Agency reported that the earthquake occurred at approximately 10:06 a.m. local time, with a depth of 50 kilometers.

data.title: Hong Kong Robotics Stocks Surge as Market Gains Momentum
data.description: According to Jin10, Hong Kong's robotics concept stocks experienced a notable surge. UBTECH Robotics (09880.HK) saw an increase of over 10%, Hesai Technology (02525.HK) rose by more than 7%, Hua Hong Semiconductor (01347.HK) climbed over 6%, Yunji Technology (02670.HK) gained more than 5%, and Horizon Robotics (09660.HK) advanced by over 3%. This collective strengthening of robotics stocks highlights growing investor interest and confidence in the sector.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 68,000 USDT with a 0.56% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 01, 2026, 01:35 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 68,000 USDT and is now trading at 67,928.390625 USDT, with a narrowed 0.56% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Polymarket responds to high fee controversy: Admits calculation error and has fixed it; abnormal fees have been removed.
data.description: The prediction market platform Polymarket announced on Discord that, in response to community concerns about excessively high fees in certain categories, an adjustment made to optimize the fee curve mistakenly used USD-denominated taker volume. This has now been changed to calculation based on "number of shares," an industry standard that effectively eliminates fee imbalances. The team stated that in the weather and economics markets, the incorrect calculation method, coupled with additional indices, caused severe distortions in the fee curve, particularly in low price ranges (such as 0.1 cents), resulting in abnormally high fees. This was the main reason for the recent controversy surrounding "super high fees" on social media. The platform has now revised its fee table and removed the relevant indices, restoring the fee structure to normal for all markets. Furthermore, the team suggests that users sensitive to fees can achieve fee-free trading by placing limit orders. Additionally, with the latest update, users can also receive a 20% to 25% maker rebate incentive.

data.title: Galaxy Digital launches SOL staking function
data.description: Nasdaq-listed crypto financial services group Galaxy Digital announced the launch of its SOL staking feature. The service, available through its fintech platform GalaxyOne, is open to eligible customers in over 40 US states and territories. All SOL staked on GalaxyOne will be delegated to Galaxy Digital validators. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description:
1. The first batch of compliant stablecoin licenses in Hong Kong has been delayed; the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) responded that it is making every effort to expedite the process.
2. US stocks closed with a broad rally in the crypto sector, with ABTC rising over 17.01%.
3. Bitcoin did not experience a six-month consecutive decline; its March return reached 1.62%.
4. The White House is assessing contingency plans for oil prices exceeding $150, and may use emergency powers to stabilize fuel costs.
5. Two newly created wallets withdrew 9,519 ETH from WhiteBit.
6. The US CFTC is closely monitoring abnormal trading in the crude oil futures market.
7. US lawmakers have proposed the PREDICT Act, which aims to prohibit the president and members of Congress from participating in prediction markets.
8. The US Secretary of State stated that the end line of the war with Iran is visible.
9. Bloomberg analysts reported a large influx of investors into US Treasury ETFs, suggesting that cash may become the "last safe haven."
10. Solana DEX trading volume fell to its lowest level since September 2024, with the market focusing on the $80 support level.

data.title: Hong Kong Stablecoin Licenses Delayed Amid Market Anticipation
data.description: Hong Kong's anticipated issuance of its first batch of compliant stablecoin issuer licenses, initially expected in March 2026, has yet to occur. According to NS3.AI, a spokesperson for the Hong Kong Monetary Authority stated that the authority is actively progressing with the licensing process and plans to announce the results publicly at a suitable time. Market attention is primarily directed towards HSBC and Standard Chartered for the initial batch of licenses, while applications for the second batch are also in progress.

data.title: U.S. Stocks and Crypto-Linked Shares Surge
data.description: U.S. stocks ended the trading session on a positive note, with significant gains observed across major indices. According to NS3.AI, the Dow Jones Industrial Average increased by 2.49%, while the S&P 500 climbed 2.91%. The Nasdaq Composite also saw a substantial rise of 3.83%. In the cryptocurrency sector, ABTC experienced a notable increase of 17.01%, contributing to the overall positive performance of crypto-linked shares.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Avoid Consecutive Monthly Declines in March
data.description: Bitcoin experienced a 1.62% increase in March, successfully avoiding a sixth consecutive monthly decline. According to NS3.AI, Ethereum also saw a rise of 6.97%, preventing a seventh straight monthly drop. Coinglass data reveals that for the quarter, Bitcoin recorded a return of -22.34%, while Ethereum posted a return of -29.26%. Both cryptocurrencies marked their third-largest quarterly declines in the tracked period.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,100 USDT with a Narrowed 2.24% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 01, 2026, 01:27 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,100 USDT and is now trading at 2,099.909912 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.24% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bloomberg analysts: Investors flock to US Treasury ETFs, cash may become the "last safe haven"
data.description: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, stated on the X platform that amid a decline in US stocks and a lackluster performance in gold (the failure of their supposed "zero correlation" has surprised the market), investors are flocking to US Treasury ETFs. Net inflows into US Treasury ETFs in March reached approximately $30 billion, more than double the recent monthly average, primarily concentrated in ultra-short-term products such as SGOV and BIL. However, the current market seems to lack suitable "safe-haven assets," making holding cash and patiently observing a better strategy. Previously, it was reported that Buffett disclosed Berkshire Hathaway purchased $17 billion worth of US Treasury bonds this week.

data.title: Foreign Central Banks Reduce U.S. Treasury Holdings Amid Economic Strain
data.description:
Foreign central banks have reduced their holdings of U.S. Treasuries to the lowest level since 2012 to support their economies and currencies during the ongoing conflict in Iran. According to BlockBeats, the value of Treasuries held by official institutions at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York has decreased by $82 billion since February 25.

Some central banks have intervened in foreign exchange markets to bolster their national currencies, often involving the sale of U.S. dollars to address crises. Since February 27, the Central Bank of Turkey alone has sold $22 billion worth of foreign government bonds.

Analysts indicate that supply shocks have placed significant pressure on global central banks.

data.title: Indian Rupee Faces Historic Low Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict, Analysts Warn
data.description: According to Jin10, the Indian rupee could fall to a historic low of 100 against the U.S. dollar if the conflict in Iran continues. Despite efforts by Indian authorities over the past year to mitigate the rupee's approximately 10% decline, strategists warn these measures may only offer temporary relief. Analysts from Wells Fargo indicate that rising oil prices could exacerbate inflation and the current account deficit, accelerating the rupee's depreciation. Options market pricing supports this view, suggesting expectations for the rupee to weaken further towards the 100 mark. As one of Asia's worst-performing currencies this year, the rupee's ongoing decline has prompted the Reserve Bank of India to implement its most stringent measures in over a decade, capping banks' end-of-day positions in the onshore currency market at $100 million. This move forces banks to scale back and limit large unilateral bets on the rupee. However, Monday's market performance highlighted the limitations of this measure, as the rupee initially surged 1.4% due to regulatory actions but quickly reversed to a new low of 95.125. Options pricing indicates traders see a 13% probability of the rupee breaching 100 by the end of June, with this likelihood rising to 41% by year-end.

data.title: Solana DEX trading volume fell to its lowest level since September 2024, with the market focusing on the $80 support level.
data.description: Solana's native token, SOL, fell approximately 11% after encountering resistance at $93 last Wednesday, significantly underperforming the overall crypto market and repeatedly testing the $80 support level. Meanwhile, Solana's network fees have declined for two consecutive months, raising market concerns that its price may further retest the $75 level. On-chain data shows that Solana's current total value locked (TVL) is approximately $6.3 billion, still significantly lagging behind Ethereum's $54.1 billion. However, Solana's network fees have remained 80% higher over the past 30 days, primarily due to Ethereum's reduction of mainnet fees through Layer 2 Rollups and data blobs. Specifically, Solana's network fees fell to $18.5 million in March, a 42% decrease from $30 million in January, mainly due to the shrinking trading volume on decentralized exchanges (DEXs). Data shows that Solana DEX trading volume fell to $55.5 billion, the lowest level since September 2024. In contrast, Ethereum's DEX trading volume in March was $41 billion, a 23% decrease from two months prior. However, when considering Layer 2 networks such as Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, and Optimism, its DEX market share has risen from 33% in January to 42%, challenging Solana's dominance and explaining SOL's current downward pressure. Nevertheless, Solana's ecosystem fundamentals remain supportive. In the past 30 days, Solana boasted 13 DApps with revenues exceeding $1 million, surpassing Ethereum's 11 and BNB Chain and Base's 4 each. Protocol revenues, including those from projects like Helium Network, continue to attract developer and funding attention. Overall, while declining DEX activity is dragging down short-term performance, Solana's ecosystem profitability and developer appeal remain strong, and there is insufficient evidence to suggest that SOL will necessarily fall below the $75 support level due to declining network fees. (Cointelegraph)
